content: There have been quite a few patches related to 64-bit division. Could we instead provide a definition of __aeabi_uldivmod in terms of div64_s64?

From my understanding:
Yes we could technically do that, but we don't want to since this operation is very expensive on 32 bit architectures (and a full 64 bit by 64 bit division is usually not needed).

Implementing __aeabi_uldivmod would make this operation implicit and easy to use accidentally where it is not actually needed. It wasn't implemented in the first place because of this, since the same applies to C.
I think all the CONFIG_64BIT workarounds that I think are now in multiple places in the kernel are also pretty bad.
On the full 64by64 bit division point, does arm have 64by32 bit division?
There is the generic version, plus arm32 appears to have an optimized version from a quick look (__div64_32).
Going to bump this thread again - I think we should really figure out something for this, as I just ran into another 64 bit divide in one of the patch series for booting up the GSP firmware on nova

Something I've been curious about - is it at all possible that we could implement __aeabi_uldivmod but somehow make it so that Div isn't implemented by default for 64 bit users so that they have to use a named function?
No, we can't remove impls like that.
Have we had any other ideas then I guess regarding how we want to expose this in the future?
I mean, a band-aid is to just add a method and call it.
but it won't prevent you from using /

So I'm curious, I guess I would have assumed that if we're only using core rust that we would potentially be able to make changes specific to the linux kernel. or is that just something we're trying to avoid in general?

Alice Ryhl said:

I mean, a band-aid is to just add a method and call it.

mhm, we'll likely need a helper function here as well since the function I'm looking at is a macro, DIV_U64_ROUND_UP
We can't change core. It's shipped with the compiler, not with Linux.
ahhh gotcha, that makes sense

We can raise it again to the upstream Rust people to see what they say. I am not sure, in any case, if we would want the / operator to do that by default, i.e. do we want to force users to be explicit for that case?

Miguel Ojeda said:

We can raise it again to the upstream Rust people to see what they say. I am not sure, in any case, if we would want the / operator to do that by default, i.e. do we want to force users to be explicit for that case?

Perhaps we could have a special error message when it gets used that points people towards the correct functions, and points out that 64 bit division should be avoided by default if possible. of course that's something we'd need to somehow request of the core library though
Clippy has a similar lint: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/pull/12451
but it's for all division, not just 64-bit

Sounds like we could make the case pretty easily then, though I'm curious then how we would handle things like div_ceil(), since I'd at least assume we would prefer to use the kernel math library for this sort of thing?
or would we be OK with using rust's version of these things
it's probably fine
JFYI: seems I was unaware until now that nova actually made a decision at some point to depend on CONFIG_64BIT, so we at least don't need to worry about that usecase
What about leaving __aeabi_uldivmod undefined so that it causes a linker error?
